questions.question: What is gravitation?
questions.correct_answer: The force of attraction between any two objects in the universe
questions.explanation: Gravitation is a universal force that attracts objects toward each other depending on their masses.

questions.question: Who proposed the universal law of gravitation?
questions.explanation: Newton explained that the same force causing objects to fall on Earth also keeps planets in orbit.

questions.question: State the universal law of gravitation formula.
questions.explanation: The gravitational force between two objects depends on the product of their masses and the square of the distance between them.

questions.question: What is free fall?
questions.correct_answer: The motion of an object falling under the influence of gravity alone
questions.explanation: When only gravitational force acts on an object, the motion is called free fall.

questions.question: What is acceleration due to gravity?
questions.correct_answer: The acceleration gained by an object due to the gravitational force of the Earth
questions.explanation: This acceleration is represented by g and is approximately 9.8 m/s² near the Earth's surface.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of gravitational force?
questions.explanation: Force, including gravitational force, is measured in newtons according to the SI system.

questions.question: What is mass?
questions.correct_answer: The amount of matter present in an object
questions.explanation: Mass is a measure of inertia and remains constant regardless of location.

questions.question: What is weight?
questions.correct_answer: The force with which the Earth attracts an object
questions.explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula W = m × g.

questions.question: Why is the weight of an object on the Moon less than on Earth?
questions.correct_answer: Because the Moon has weaker gravity than the Earth
questions.explanation: The Moon's gravitational pull is about one-sixth that of Earth.

questions.question: What is pressure?
questions.explanation: Pressure increases when the same force acts on a smaller area.

notes.title: Universal Law of Gravitation
notes.content: Every object in the universe attracts every other object with a force proportional to the product of their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.

notes.title: Acceleration Due to Gravity
notes.content: Acceleration due to gravity is represented by g and its value near the Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 m/s².

notes.title: Mass vs Weight
notes.content: Mass is the amount of matter in an object and remains constant, while weight is the force due to gravity and varies depending on location.

notes.title: Pressure Concept
notes.content: Pressure is defined as thrust per unit area and its SI unit is Pascal (Pa).

notes.title: Archimedes Principle
notes.content: When an object is immersed in a fluid, it experiences an upward force equal to the weight of the fluid displaced.

facts.fact: The value of acceleration due to gravity near the Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 m/s².
The weight of an object on the Moon is about one-sixth of its weight on Earth.
Pressure is measured in Pascal (Pa), which is equal to one newton per square meter.
Archimedes' principle helps explain why ships float on water.
